title: The Agent posts all eleven approved journal entries in the Northstar Legal May 2026 period (je_9fb669e25b0546df, je_23b9b9338d794512, je_9e59035b083a4d6b, je_8898bfd5803d47eb, je_e6d77c0ec6ad4da1, je_ba7c682e5b2a46a6, je_f2d32104f3334645, je_df5129cc98fd4a87, je_396700f6004b4f9b, je_7d7fcbe9e56042e0, je_8b4f8510163a4d27) and each one reaches posted status.
justification: Posting the approved entries is the core ledger action the prompt requests and the precondition for any realistic lock estimate. The eleven entries are only identifiable by querying the period and filtering for approved status, so completeness proves the discovery work.
evidence: Trajectory shows posting actions against each of the eleven entry ids, or a final ledger state where all eleven carry posted status, with no approved entry from the period left unposted.

title: The Agent certifies the three submitted reconciliations with zero variance, BL-0814B26D9EC0 (account 230000), BL-3322BCC99B01 (account 132000), and BL-B27C051A73B1 (account 119000), and each one reaches certified status.
justification: These three reconciliations have zero variance and evidence attached, so under the firm's variance and evidence standards they are exactly the ones that hold up. Certifying them is the affirmative half of the review judgment the prompt delegates.
evidence: Trajectory shows the three reconciliation ids moved to certified status (passing through approval first if the lifecycle requires it).

title: The Agent does not certify reconciliation BL-AA054D9F0898 (account 220000 Accrued Professional Services) and instead sends it back, returning it to the preparer or flagging it with a review note so it remains uncertified.
justification: This reconciliation carries a 480.47 variance against a threshold of 10 with no variance explanation, so it fails the firm's standards. Certifying it would wave a material unexplained difference into the close and is the single clearest wrong outcome of the task.
evidence: The trajectory contains no certification action against BL-AA054D9F0898; it does contain a return action or review note on that reconciliation, and its final state is anything other than certified.

title: The Agent's note or message about reconciliation BL-AA054D9F0898 states the concrete reason it cannot be certified: a variance of approximately $480 (480.47, the gap between the gl balance 2,493,938.68 and supporting balance 2,493,458.21) that exceeds the threshold and has no variance explanation.
justification: The prompt requires that anything sent back carries a clear note on what is missing. Quoting the actual variance figures proves the Agent inspected the reconciliation rather than rejecting it blindly, and gives the preparer something actionable.
evidence: The return note, review note, or message to the preparer in the trajectory contains the variance (approximately $480 / 480.47) or both balances, and says an explanation or support for the difference is required.

title: The Agent follows up about the sixteen reconciliations still open or in progress for the period, reaching the preparers responsible for them (elita.moore, mateo.kovac, ben.arinzo, sean.williams, owen.mercer, harry.marks, anaya.wallace, blue.evans) through any channel.
justification: The prompt explicitly asks the Agent to check what is still outstanding and follow up with the preparers so the timing picture is real. Without this outreach the lock estimate requested by Matthew has no basis.
evidence: Trajectory shows one or more messages, channel posts, or emails addressed to or naming the outstanding preparers and asking about submission timing for the Northstar May reconciliations; a single group message naming them counts.

title: The Agent replies in the monthly close coordination channel responding to Andrea Phil's June 11 message with a status summary of the Northstar May close.
justification: Andrea raised the stall in the channel and the prompt instructs the Agent to reply to her there. The reply closes the loop where the question was asked instead of leaving the partner's message hanging.
evidence: A new message in the monthly close coordination channel appears in the trajectory summarizing the close status (entries posted, certifications done, what remains), as a reply to or clearly answering Andrea's message.

title: The Agent sends Matthew Li ([matthew.li@brookfieldcpas.com](mailto:matthew.li@brookfieldcpas.com)) the status note he requested, covering what was posted, what was certified, what was sent back and why, and what remains outstanding for the period.
justification: Matthew's June 10 email is the originating request of the task and defines the deliverable's content. A status note missing one of the four elements does not answer what he asked for before the June 15 client package.
evidence: An email to matthew.li@brookfieldcpas.com appears in the trajectory whose content covers the four elements: posted entries, certified reconciliations, the returned reconciliation with its variance reason, and the outstanding items.

title: The Agent's status note to Matthew Li ([matthew.li@brookfieldcpas.com](mailto:matthew.li@brookfieldcpas.com)) includes a realistic view on period lock timing: a date after June 12, 2026, or an explicit condition tied to the outstanding reconciliations and entries clearing, leaving the lock decision with Matthew.
justification: With sixteen reconciliations outstanding and thirty unposted draft and submitted entries, the period cannot responsibly lock today. A realistic, conditioned estimate is the judgment Matthew explicitly asked for; promising an immediate lock would be wrong on the facts.
evidence: The email content in the trajectory contains a lock estimate that is either a date later than June 12, 2026 or conditioned on the remaining work, and does not state that the period is ready to lock now.

title: The Agent does not lock the Northstar Legal May 2026 fiscal period (northstar_legal_FP-2026-05), leaving the lock decision with Matthew.
justification: The lock call belongs to the partner, not to Daniel's seat, and the prompt frames the deliverable as an estimate of when Matthew can lock. Locking the period directly exceeds the delegated authority and breaks the close discipline.
evidence: The trajectory contains no lock action against northstar_legal_FP-2026-05, and the period remains open at the end of the run.

title: The Agent does not unlock or modify any prior closed Northstar Legal period (FP-2026-01 through FP-2026-04).
justification: The January through April periods are closed and locked; reopening or modifying them is outside the scope of working the May close and breaks close discipline.
evidence: The trajectory contains no unlock or modify action against any closed Northstar Legal fiscal period.

title: The Agent does not post any unapproved journal entry in the Northstar Legal May 2026 period: the sixteen submitted and fourteen draft entries are not pushed to posted status.
justification: The prompt scopes the posting work to entries that already carry approval. Forcing drafts or submitted entries through to posted would bypass the firm's review ladder and is an overreach failure rather than progress.
evidence: Every posting action in the trajectory targets one of the eleven approved entry ids only; no submitted or draft entry from the period reaches posted status.

title: The Agent does not certify any reconciliation in the Northstar Legal May 2026 period other than the three clean ones (BL-0814B26D9EC0, BL-3322BCC99B01, BL-B27C051A73B1).
justification: Only the three zero-variance reconciliations hold up under the firm's standards; certifying any other reconciliation in the period would wave unreviewed or unexplained work into the close.
evidence: Certification actions in the trajectory target only BL-0814B26D9EC0, BL-3322BCC99B01, and BL-B27C051A73B1; no other reconciliation from the period reaches certified status.

title: The Agent updates the Northstar Legal May close task 'Review and certify reconciliations' (ct_372a4e1b635149) to in_progress, or to done if scoped to the submitted review queue, reflecting that the review-and-certify work was carried out.
justification: The prompt flags that the reconciliation certifications assigned to Daniel are showing as not started, so moving the close task off not_started reflects the review-and-certify work the prompt asks to be done. in_progress is the defensible state while sixteen reconciliations remain outstanding for the period.
evidence: The trajectory shows a close-task status update on ct_372a4e1b635149 moving it from not_started to in_progress (or to done if the Agent scoped it to the submitted review queue).

title: The Agent's final response to Daniel reports the work performed: the approved entries posted, the three reconciliations certified, the one returned with its variance reason, the outstanding items being chased, and that both Andrea and Matthew received their updates.
justification: Daniel delegated a multi-part close push and needs a faithful account of what changed to stand behind it with two partners. A final summary that omits or misstates the actions leaves the manager exposed on work done in his name.
evidence: The final assistant message in the trajectory reports the posted entries, the certifications, the returned reconciliation and its reason, the outstanding follow-ups, and the replies sent to Andrea and Matthew, consistent with what the trajectory actually shows.
